author: tmiychao
content:
Share your preliminary updates in this thread. Make sure to also post it to [github](https://github.com/eth-protocol-fellows/cohort-seven/blob/main/development-updates.md). This is just an initial opportunity to get ready for the fellowship, share your plans and practice writing your updates. 
Do not use LLMs to generate your updates but communicate your actual thoughts and plans please, slop updates are not accepted in the repo.

author: xrs_01
content:
Thank you Mario. Gm.

I'll be working on a holistic research approach to Lean Execution. I'm currently working on a research paper to serve as a guideline to myself. I have a number of ideas I'm currently doing preliminary research for, but I plan on practically grounding any research in specifications and actual proof-of-concepts later down the line. I'd also like to contribute to the Lean Ethereum development space in ways outside of my own ideas.

What I'm working on roughly falls into the sub-topics of SNARKs, zkEVMs, sharding, and statelessness.

<https://docs.fileverse.io/d/020011100018#k=8xrRiRcxDIewJk6a5D1sKgPAOrwnot0ONS683iTxzGs8>

author: parthsingh2006
content:
hey guys, we were waiting for this announcement for a long time 
me and my friend <@1335660981019480075>  applied for the cohort but we didn't get selected so we'll be working permissionlessly. we had started our work after the project list was updated, and one which particularly matched with our skillset was the reth partial statelessness prototype.
we both have been studying about all the theory that we can around it, the historical contexts, eips, discussion forums etc... so for the next step, we plan to do some experiments by running some nodes and observing the implementation in real time.. 

we have already gained quite an understanding of the topic and have made some rough plans on how to approach the project too which we'll post on GitHub soon!
looking forward to working alongside the cohort and would appreciate any suggestions throughout the program.

Thankyou!!

content: cheers, see yall throughout the cohort 🍻

author: krisoshea.eth
content:
Hi everyone, I'm Kris, a software engineer based in Dublin Ireland. Looking forward to meeting you all. 

With respect to my fellowship project, I would like to work on the Reth partial statefullness and state expiry prototype.

I understand this is quite a large project with a lot of subcomponents, but I've started to look into it over the last 10 days and I've gotten pretty comfortable with how Reth works and how the codebase is organised. I've also looked into the latest updates to Reth and the relevant EIPs related to this project.

There seems to be a lot of subparts to this project, so I'm breaking them down into their individual components and I've been researching all of them at length. There are also a lot of different ways of approaching this problem so I’ve been mapping them into key buckets / solution types and hope to discuss them at length to determine what is the best approach and what would be achievable within the 5 month timeline.

it seems like there are some interesting developments in the Geth and Erigon clients in terms of achieving this partial statefulness goal. And there's a recent EIP around BALs that directly addresses some issues around partial statefulness with the current implementation of BALs.

I look forward to researching this in more detail, and I'll be posting a more comprehensive update on GitHub later this week once I've finished conducting my initial research and writing this up properly.

Very keen to meet you all. I can't wait to get started. Thanks!

author: zemse
content: Hey everyone, I would like to work on Ream's Execution Layer Integration for the Lean Chain. I've have previously made some small PR contributions into Ream and now exploring this one. Excited to work along with everyone! Lesgo!

author: astrion0587
content: Hello everyone, I am Astrion, I would like to work on the topic of `Reth: Partial Statefulness and State Expiry Prototype`. I am currently reading the EIP-7928 to try and gain a deeper understanding about BALs in general and how they can be integrated here. After that I'll start looking into the Reth codebase properly. I can already see a lot of interest regarding this specific project, so I hope to collaborate with all the other participants. Thanks :)
